     The Numbers Business Report

Are You There God? It’s Me, Margaret (2023)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $20,372,406Details
International Box Office $1,209,418
Worldwide Box Office $21,581,824
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $565,799
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $1,173,567
Total $1,739,366
Further financial details...

Synopsis

For over fifty years, Judy Blume’s classic Are You There God? It’s Me, Margaret. has connected with readers through its relatable story, achingly funny details, and candid exploration of life’s biggest questions. In Lionsgate’s big-screen adaptation, 11-year-old Margaret is uprooted from her life in New York City for the suburbs of New Jersey, going through the messy and tumultuous throes of puberty with new friends in a new school. She relies on her mother, Barbara, who is also struggling to adjust to life outside the big city, and her adoring grandmother, Sylvia, who isn’t happy they moved away and likes to remind them every chance she gets.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$6,739,037 (33.1% of total gross)
Legs:3.02 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:94.4% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$30,000,000 (worldwide box office is 0.7 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,343 opening theaters/3,343 max. theaters, 3.4 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $22,271,840

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: April 28th, 2023 (Wide) by Lionsgate
International Releases: May 4th, 2023 (Wide), released as Crescendo Juntas (Brazil)
May 19th, 2023 (Wide) (United Kingdom)
September 7th, 2023 (Wide), released as ¿Estás ahí, Dios? Soy yo, Margaret (Mexico)
Video Release: June 6th, 2023 by Lionsgate Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for thematic material involving sexual education and some suggestive material.
(Rating bulletin 2777 (cert #53511), 4/5/2023)
Running Time: 105 minutes
Filming location (country): United States
Filming location (state or county): North Carolina, United States
Geographical setting: New Jersey
Plot point: Christians, Coming of Age, Jewish
Source material: Young Adult Book Adaptation
Subgenre: Comedy Drama
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Comedy
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Historical Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Gracie Films, Lionsgate
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

For over fifty years, Judy Blume’s classic Are You There God? It’s Me, Margaret. has connected with readers through its relatable story, achingly funny details, and candid exploration of life’s biggest questions. In Lionsgate’s big-screen adaptation, 11-year-old Margaret is uprooted from her life in New York City for the suburbs of New Jersey, going through the messy and tumultuous throes of puberty with new friends in a new school. She relies on her mother, Barbara, who is also struggling to adjust to life outside the big city, and her adoring grandmother, Sylvia, who isn’t happy they moved away and likes to remind them every chance she gets.
